UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores information about a user (student or faculty) who can access the library system, including their institutional identity, role, and current status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

institutionalEmail.description: The institutional email address of the user, primarily used for login and identification.

role.description: The assigned role of the user within the system, either 'Administrator' or 'User' (student/faculty).

isBlocked.description: A flag indicating whether the user is currently blocked from accessing the library system by an administrator.

firstName.description: The first name of the user for personalization and identification.

lastName.description: The last name of the user for personalization and identification.

collegeId.description: Reference to the College entity the user is affiliated with. (Relationship: College 1:N UserProfile)

College.title: College
College.description: Represents an academic college or department within the institution to which students and faculty belong.

id.description: Unique identifier for the College entity.

name.description: The official name of the college or department (e.g., 'College of Engineering', 'College of Education').

VisitReason.title: VisitReason
VisitReason.description: Defines predefined or categorized reasons for a user's visit to the library, supporting structured data input and statistical reporting.

id.description: Unique identifier for the VisitReason entity.

name.description: A descriptive name for the reason of the library visit (e.g., 'Study', 'Research', 'Borrow Books', 'Use Computer').

category.description: A broader categorization of the visit reason, potentially assigned or refined by an AI tool (e.g., 'Academic Support', 'Leisure Reading', 'Administrative').

LibraryVisit.title: LibraryVisit
LibraryVisit.description: Records a single instance of a user entering and potentially exiting the library, capturing details about the visit.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LibraryVisit entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity of the individual who made the visit. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N LibraryVisit)

checkInTime.description: The date and time when the user checked into the library.

checkOutTime.description: The date and time when the user checked out of the library. This field is optional as recording exit might be a secondary feature.

reasonId.description: Reference to the VisitReason entity explaining the primary purpose of this library visit. (Relationship: VisitReason 1:N LibraryVisit)

freeTextReason.description: The verbatim reason provided by the user for their visit, before any categorization by an AI tool or selection from predefined reasons.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. The 'userId' wildcard matches the authenticated user's UID for path-based security. Includes 'role' and 'isBlocked' fields for authorization. Allows self-management by users and full management by administrators.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores static information about academic colleges. This collection is primarily for lookup and can be read by all authenticated users. Only administrators can create, update, or delete entries.

params.name: collegeId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific college.

definition.description: Stores predefined reasons for library visits. This collection is for lookup and can be read by all authenticated users. Only administrators can create, update, or delete entries, and potentially manage AI categorization hints.

params.name: reasonId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific visit reason.

definition.description: Records each library visit, nested under the owning user's profile. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence, crucial for Collection Group Queries by administrators and for secure self-access without relying on parent document lookups.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who made the visit, matching the parent UserProfile document ID.

params.name: visitId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific library visit record.

definition.description: A dedicated collection to signify administrative users (DBAC). The existence of a document with a user's UID in this collection grants global administrative privileges. Used for 'existence' checks in security rules to ensure Authorization Independence and enforce global roles.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who holds an administrative role, matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to adhere strictly to the core design principles and strategy mandates, prioritizing Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC, QAPs, and Invariants.

**Authorization Independence (Denormalization Strategy):**
1.  **UserProfile:** Each `UserProfile` document is a top-level document, where the document ID (`userId`) directly corresponds to `request.auth.uid`. This ensures that access rules for a user's own profile (read/update) can be evaluated without any `get()` calls to parent documents. The `role` and `isBlocked` fields are embedded directly within the user's profile, allowing direct attribute-based authorization checks.
2.  **LibraryVisit:** These documents are stored in a subcollection under the respective `UserProfile` (`/users/{userId}/libraryVisits/{visitId}`). Crucially, each `LibraryVisit` document explicitly includes the `userId` field (as defined in the schema). This denormalization of the owner's ID within the `LibraryVisit` document itself is paramount. It allows authorization rules for `LibraryVisit` (especially for Collection Group Queries by administrators) to check `resource.data.userId` without needing to perform a `get()` operation on the parent `UserProfile` document. This eliminates hierarchical authorization dependencies, enabling atomic operations and simplifying rule logic.
3.  **Colleges & VisitReasons:** These collections hold static lookup data. Their authorization (read-only for all authenticated users, admin-write) is entirely self-contained and does not depend on data from other documents.
4.  **Admin Roles:** A dedicated top-level collection, `/roles_admin/{userId}`, is used for defining system administrators. An administrator's status is determined by the *existence* of a document with their UID as the ID in this collection. This 'existence over content' approach allows for a highly efficient and independent security check using `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))` in security rules, completely avoiding `get()` calls on any other user or profile data to determine administrative privileges.

**QAPs (Secure List Operations):**
1.  **UserProfile:** As a top-level collection, administrators can perform efficient `list` operations (`get` and `list` methods) without needing to apply document-specific filters in rules that would require `get()` calls. Users can only `get` their own profile, which is efficient by path.
2.  **Colleges & VisitReasons:** These are also top-level collections. Since they are designed for read-only access for all authenticated users (and admin-write), `list` operations are inherently efficient and secure as no row-level filtering is required by the rules.
3.  **LibraryVisit (Subcollection & Collection Group Query):**
    *   For individual users, listing their own visits is efficient and secure due to the path-based ownership: `/users/$(request.auth.uid)/libraryVisits`. The query is naturally scoped to the user's data.
    *   For administrators, viewing all library visits across all users requires a Collection Group Query on `libraryVisits`. The explicit `userId` field within *each* `LibraryVisit` document (the denormalized owner ID) is critical here. This ensures that the security rule for the Collection Group Query can grant access to administrators based solely on their admin status (`exists(/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))`) without needing to perform `get()` operations on the parent `UserProfile` documents to verify ownership or permissions, thus maintaining QAPs and authorization independence for these broader `list` operations.
